author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew IX. 27-30.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And when Jesus departed thence, two blind men followed Him, crying, and saying, Thou Son of David, have mercy on us.And when He was come into the house, the blind men came to Him: and Jesus saith unto them, Believe ye that I am able to do this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They say unto Him, Yea, Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then touched He their eyes, saying, According to your faith be itunto you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And their eyes were opened.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore can it be that He puts them off,and they crying out?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Here again teaching us utterly to repel the glory that cometh from the multitude.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For because the house was near, He leads them thither to heal them in private.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this is evident from the fact, that He charged them moreover to tell no man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But this is no light charge against the Jews; when these men, though their eyes were struck out, receive the faith by hearing alone, but they beholding the miracles, and having their sight to witness what was happening, do all just contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see their earnestness also, both by their cry, and by their prayer itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they did not merely approach Him, but with loud cries, and alleging nought else but “mercy.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they called Him “Son of David,” because the name was thought to be honorable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In many passages, for instance, did the prophetslikewise so call the kings, whom they wished to honor, and to declare great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And having brought them into the house, He puts to them a further question.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in many cases He made a point of healing on entreaty, lest any should suppose Him to be rushingupon these miracles through vainglory: and not on this account alone, but to indicate also that they deserve healing, and that no one should say, “If it was of mere mercy that He saved, all men ought to be saved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For even His love to man hath a kind of proportion; depending on the faith of them that are healed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not for these causes only doth He require faith of them, but forasmuch as they called Him “Son of David,” He to lead them up to what is higher, and to teach them to entertain the imaginations they ought of Himself, saith, “Believe ye that I am able to do this?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He did not say, “Believe ye that I am able to entreat my Father, that I am able to pray” but, “that I am able to do this?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is their word?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Yea, Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They call Him no more Son of David, but soar higher, and acknowledge His dominion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And then at last He for His part lays His hand upon them, saying, “According to your faith be it unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this He doth to confirm their faith, and to show that they are participators in the good work, and to witness that their words were not words of flattery.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither did He say, “Let your eyes be opened,” but, “According to your faith be it unto you;” which He saith to many of them that came unto Him; before the healing of their bodies, hastening to proclaim the faith in their soul; so as both to make them more approved, and to render others more serious.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus with respect to the sick of the palsy also; for there too before giving nerve to the body, He raises up the fallen soul, saying, “Son, be of good cheer, thy sins be forgiven thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the young damsel too, when He had raised her up, He detained, and by the food taught her her Benefactor; and in the case of the centurion also He did in like manner, leaving the whole to his faith; and as to His disciples again, when delivering them from the storm on the sea, He delivered them first from their want of faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Just so likewise in this case: He knew indeed, even before their cry, the secrets of their mind; but that He might lead on others also to the same earnestness, He makes them known to the rest as well, by the result of their cure proclaiming their hidden faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then after their cure He commands them to tell no man; neither doth He merely command them, but with much strictness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For Jesus,” it is said, “straitly charged them, saying, See that no man know it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But they, when they were departed, spread abroad His fame in all that country.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They however did not endure this, but became preachers, and evangelists; and when bidden to hide what had been done, they endured it not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if in another place we find Him saying, “Go thy way, and declare the glory of God,”that is not contrary to this, but even highly in agreement herewith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He instructs us to say nothing ourselves, concerning ourselves, but even to forbid them that would eulogise us: but if the glory be referred to God, then not only not to forbid, but to command men to do this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And as they went out,” it is said, “behold, they brought unto Him a dumb man possessed with a devil.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the affliction was not natural, but the device of the evil spirit; wherefore also he needs others to bring him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he could neither make entreaty himself, being speechless, nor supplicate others, when the evil spirit had bound his tongue, and together with his tongue had fettered his soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause neither doth He require faith of him, but straightway heals the disease.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For when the devil was cast out,” it saith, “the dumb spake: and the multitudes marvelled, saying, It was never so seen in Israel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now this especially vexed the Pharisees, that they preferred Him to all, not only that then were, but that had ever been.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they preferred Him, not for His healing, but for His doing it easily and quickly, and to diseases innumerable and incurable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And thus the multitude; but the Pharisees quite contrariwise; not only disparaging the works, but saying things contradictory to themselves, and not ashamed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such a thing is wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what say they?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “He casteth out devils through the prince of the devils.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What can be more foolish than this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in the first place, as He also saith further on, it is impossible that a devil should cast out a devil, for that being is wont to repair what belongs to himself, not to pull it down.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He did not cast out devils only, but also cleansed lepers, and raised the dead, and curbed the sea, and remitted sins, and preached the kingdom, and brought men unto the Father; things which a demon would never either choose, or at any time be able to effect.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the devils bring men to idols, and withdraw them from God, and persuade them to disbelieve the life to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The devil doth not bestow kindness when he is insulted; forasmuch as even when not insulted, he harms those that court and honor him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He doeth the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For after these their insults and revilings,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “He went about,” it is said, “all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every sickness and every disease.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And so far from punishing them for their insensibility, He did not even simply rebuke them; at once both evincing His meekness, and so refuting the calumny; and at the same time minded also by the signs which followed to exhibit His proof more completely: and then to adduce also the refutation by words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He went about therefore both in cities, and in countries, and in their synagogues; instructing us to requite our calumniators, not with fresh calumnies, but with greater benefits.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since, if not for man’s sake, but God’s, thou doest good to thy fellow-servants; whatsoever they may do, leave not thou off doing them good, that thy reward may be greater; since he surely, who upon their calumny leaves off his doing good, signifies that for their praise’ sake, not for God’s sake, he applies himself to that kind of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause Christ, to teach us that of mere goodness He had entered on this, so far from waiting for the sick to come to Him, of Himself hastened unto them, bearing them two of the greatest blessings; one, the gospel of the kingdom; another, the perfect cure of all their diseases.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not a city did He overlook, not a village did He hasten by, but visited every place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not even at this doth He stop, but He exhibits also another instance of His forethought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “When He saw,” it is said, “the multitudes, He was moved with compassion on them, because they were troubled,and scattered abroad, as sheep having no shepherd.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then saith He unto His disciples, The harvest truly is plenteous, but the laborers are few, pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that He will send forth laborers into His harvest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See again His freedom from vainglory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That He may not draw all men unto Himself, He sends out His disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not with this view only, but that He might also teach them, after practising in Palestine, as in a sort of training-school, to strip themselves for their conflicts with the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this purpose then He makes the exercises even more serious than the actual conflicts, so far as pertained to their own virtue; that they might more easily engage in the struggles that were to ensue; as it were a sort of tender nestlings whom He was at length leading out to fly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And for the present He makes them physicians of bodies, dispensing to them afterwards the cure of the soul, which is the principal thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And mark how He points out the facility and necessity of the thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “The harvest truly is plenteous, but the laborers are few.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, “not to the sowing,” saith He, “but to the reaping do I send you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which in John He expressed by, “Other men labored, and ye are entered into their labors.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And these things he said, at once repressing their pride, and preparing them to be of good courage, and signifying that the greater part of the labor came first.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And contemplate Him here too beginning from love to man, not with any requital.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For He had compassion, because they were troubled and scattered abroad as sheep having no shepherd.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This is His charge against the rulers of the Jews, that being shepherds they acted the part of wolves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so far from amending the multitude, they even marred their progress.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For instance, when they were marvelling and saying, “It was never so seen in Israel:” these were affirming the contrary, “He casteth out devils through the prince of the devils.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But of what laborers doth He speak here?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Of the twelve disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then? whereas He had said, “But the laborers are few,” did He add to their number?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By no means, but He sent them out alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore then did He say, “Pray ye the Lord of the harvest, that He wouldsend forth laborers into His harvest;” and made no addition to their number?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because though they were but twelve, He made them many from that time forward, not by adding to their number, but by giving them power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then to signify how great the gift is, He saith, “Pray ye the Lord of the harvest;” and indirectly declares it to be His own prerogative.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For after having said, “Pray ye the Lord of the harvest;” when they had not made any entreaty nor prayer, He Himself at once ordains them, reminding them also of the sayings of John,of the threshing floor, and of the Person winnowing, and of the chaff, and of the wheat.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence it is evident that Himself is the husbandman, Himself the Lord of the harvest, Himself the master and owner of the prophets.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if He sent them to reap, clearly it was not to reap what belongs to another, but what Himself had sown by the prophets.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not in this way only was He indirectly encouraging them, in calling their ministry a harvest; but also by making them able for the ministry.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And when He had called unto Him,” it saith, “His twelve disciples, He gave them power againstunclean spirits, to cast them out, and to heal all manner of sickness, and all manner of disease.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Still the Spirit was not yet given.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “there was not yet,” it saith, “a Spirit, because that Jesus was not yet glorified.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then did they cast out the spirits?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By His command, by His authority.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And mark, I pray thee, also, how well timed was the mission.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not at the beginning did He send them; but when they had enjoyed sufficiently the advantage of following Him, and had seen a dead person raised, and the sea rebuked, and devils expelled, and a paralytic new-strung, and sins remitted, and a leper cleansed, and had received a sufficient proof of His power, both by deeds and words, then He sends them forth: and not to dangerous acts, for as yet there was no danger in Palestine, but they had only to stand against evil speakings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: However, even of this He forewarns them, I mean of their perils; preparing them even before the time, and making them feel as in conflict by His continual predictions of that sort.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, since He had mentioned to us two pairs of apostles, that of Peter, and that of John, and after those had pointed out the calling of Matthew, but had said nothing to us either of the calling or of the name of the other apostles; here of necessity He sets down the list of them, and their number, and makes known their names, saying thus:

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Now the names of the twelve apostles are these; first, Simon, who is called Peter.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because there was also another Simon, the Canaanite; and there was Judas Iscariot, and Judas the brother of James; and James the son of Alphæus, and James the son of Zebedee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now Mark doth also put them according to their dignity; for after the two leaders, He then numbers Andrew; but our evangelist not so, but without distinction; or rather He sets before himself even Thomas who came far short of him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But let us look at the list of them from the beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “First, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Even this is no small praise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the one he named from his virtue, the other from his high kindred, which was in conformity to his disposition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, “James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how He arranges them not according to their dignity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to me John seems to be greater, not only than the others, but even than his brother.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this, when he had said, “Philip, and Bartholomew,” he added, “Thomas, and Matthew the Publican.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But Luke not so, but in the opposite order, and he puts him before Thomas.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Next, “James the son of Alphæus.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there was, as I have already said, the son of Zebedee also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then after having mentioned “Lebbæus, whose surname was Thaddæus,”and “Simon” Zelotes, whom he calls also “the Canaanite,” he comes to the traitor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not as a sort of enemy or foe, but as one writing a history, so hath he described him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He saith not, “the unholy, the all unholy one,” but hath named him from his city, “Judas Iscariot.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because there was also another Judas, “Lebbæus, whose surname was Thaddæus,” who, Luke saith, was the brother of James, saying, “Judas the brother of James.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore to distinguish him from this man, it saith, “Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And he is not ashamed to say, “who also betrayed Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So far were they from ever disguising aught even of those things that seem to be matters of reproach.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And first of all, and leader of the choir,is the “unlearned, the ignorant man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But let us see whither, and to whom, He sends them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “These twelve,” it is said, “Jesus sent forth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What manner of men were these?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The fishermen, the publicans: for indeed four were fishermen and two publicans, Matthew and James, and one was even a traitor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what saith He to them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He presently charges them, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Go not into the wayof the Gentiles, and into any city of the Samaritans enter ye not; but go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For think not at all,” saith He, “because they insult me, and call me demoniac, that I hate them and turn away from them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, as I sought earnestly to amend them in the first place, so keeping you away from all the rest, to them do I send you as teachers and physicians.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And I not only forbid you to preach to others before these, but I do not suffer you so much as to touch upon the road that leads thither, nor to enter into such a city.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because the Samaritans too are in a state of enmity with the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet it was an easier thing to deal with them, for they were much more favorably disposed to the faith; but the case of these was more difficult.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But for all this, He sends them on the harder task, indicating his guardian care of them, and stopping the mouths of the Jews, and preparing the way for the teaching of the apostles, that people might not hereafter blame them for “entering in to men uncircumcised,”and think they had a just cause for shunning and abhorring them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And he calls them “lost,” not “stray,” “sheep,” in every way contriving how to excuse them, and winning their mind to himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And as ye go,” saith He, “preach, saying, The kingdom of Heaven is at hand.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou the greatness of their ministry?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou the dignity of apostles?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Of nothing that is the object of sense are they commanded to speak, nor such as Moses spake of, and the prophets before them, but of some new and strange things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For while the former preached no such things, but earth, and the good things in the earth, these preached the kingdom of Heaven, and whatever is there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not from this circumstance only were these the greater, but also from their obedience: in that they shrink not, nor are they backward, like those of old;but, warned as they are of perils, and wars, and of those insupportable evils, they receive with great obedience His injunctions, as being heralds of a kingdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And what marvel,” saith one, “if having nothing to preach that is dismal or grievous, they readily obeyed?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What sayest thou? nothing grievous enjoined them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Dost thou not hear of the prisons, the executions, the civil wars, the hatred of all men? all which, He said a little while after, they must undergo.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: True, as to other men, He sent them to be procurers and heralds of innumerable blessings: but for themselves, He said and proclaimed beforehand, that they were to suffer terrible and incurable ills.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this, to make them trustworthy, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Heal the sick, cleanse the lepers,cast out devils: freely ye have received, freely give.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See how He provides for their conduct, and that no less than for their miracles, implying that the miracles without this are nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus He both quells their pride by saying, “Freely ye have received, freely give;” and takes order for their being clear of covetousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Moreover, lest it should be thought their own work,and they be lifted up by the signs that were wrought, He saith, “freely ye have received.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Ye bestow no favor on them that receive you, for not for a price did ye receive these things, nor after toil: for the grace is mine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In like manner therefore give ye to them also, for there is no finding a price worthy of them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this plucking up immediately the root of the evils, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Provide neither gold, nor silver, nor brass in your purses, nor scrip for your journey, neither two coats, neither shoes, nor yet a staff.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He said not, “take them not with you,” but, “even if you can obtain them from another, flee the evil disease.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And you see that hereby He was answering many good pur poses; first setting His disciples above suspicion; secondly, freeing them from all care, so that they might give all their leisure to the word; thirdly, teaching them His own power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Of this accordingly He quite speaks out to them afterwards, “Lacked ye anything, when I sent you naked and unshod?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He did not at once say, “Provide not,” but when He had said, “Cleanse the lepers, cast out devils,” then He said, “Provide nothing; freely ye have received, freely give;” by His way of ordering things consulting at once for their interest, their credit, and their ability.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But perhaps some one may say, that the rest may not be unaccountable, but “not to have a scrip for the journey, neither two coats, nor a staff, nor shoes,” why did He enjoin this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Being minded to train them up unto all perfection; since even further back, He had suffered them not to take thought so much as for the next day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For even to the whole world He was to send them out as teachers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore of men He makes them even angels (so to speak); releasing them from all worldly care, so that they should be possessed with one care alone, that of their teaching; or rather even from that He releases them, saying, “Take no thought how or what ye shall speak.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And thus, what seems to be very grievous and galling, this He shows to be especially light and easy for them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For nothing makes men so cheerful as being freed from anxiety and care; and especially when it is granted them, being so freed, to lack nothing, God being present, and becoming to them instead of all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Next, lest they should say, “whence then are we to obtain our necessary food?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He saith not unto them, “Ye have heard that I have told you before, ‘Behold the fowls of the air;’”(for they were not yet able to realisethis commandment in their actions); but He added what came far short of this, saying, “For the workman is worthy of his meat;”declaring that they must be nourished by their disciples, that neither they might be high minded towards those whom they were teaching, as though giving all and receiving nothing at their hands; nor these again break away, as being despised by their teachers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this, that they may not say, “Dost thou then command us to live by begging?” and be ashamed of this, He signifies the thing to be a debt, both by calling them “workmen,” and by terming what was given, “hire.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “think not,” saith He, “because the labor is in words, that the benefit conferred by you is small; nay, for the thing hath much toil; and whatsoever they that are taught may give, it is not a free gift which they bestow, but a recompence which they render: “for the workman is worthy of his meat.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But this He said, not as declaring so much to be the worth of the apostles’ labors, far from it; God forbid: but as both making it a law for them to seek nothing more, and as convincing the givers, that what they do is not an act of liberality, but a debt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And into whatsoever city or town ye shall enter, inquire who in it is worthy: and there abide till ye go thence.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, “it follows not,” saith He, “from my saying, ‘The workman is worthy of his meat,’ that I have opened to you all men’s doors: but herein also do I require you to use much circumspection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this will profit you both in respect of your credit, and for your very maintenance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if he is worthy, he will surely give you food; more especially when ye ask nothing beyond mere necessaries.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He not only requires them to seek out worthy persons, but also not to change house for house, whereby they would neither vex him that is receiving them, nor themselves get the character of gluttony and self-indulgence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this He declared by saying, “There abide till ye go thence.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this one may perceive from the other evangelists also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how He made them honorable by this also, and those that received them careful; by signifying that they rather are the gainers, both in honor, and in respect of advantage?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then pursuing again the same subject, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And when ye come into an house, salute it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if the house be worthy, let your peace come upon it; but if it be not worthy, let your peace return to you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how far He declines not to carry His injunctions?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And very fitly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as champions of godliness, and preachers to the whole world, was He training them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And in that regard disposing them to practise moderation, and making them objects of love, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear your words, when ye depart out of that house or city, shake off the dust of your feet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Verily I say unto you, it shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah in the day of judgment, than for that city.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, “do not,” saith He, “because ye are teachers, therefore wait to be saluted by others, but be first in showing that respect.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, implying that this is not a mere salutation, but a blessing, He saith, “If the house be worthy, it shall come upon it,” but if it deal insolently, its first punishment will be, not to have the benefit of your peace; and the second, that it shall suffer the doom of Sodom.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And what,” it will be said, “is their punishment to us?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Ye will have the houses of such as are worthy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what means, “Shake off the dust of your feet?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is either to signify their having received nothing of them, or to be a witness to them of the long journey, which they had travelled for their sake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark, I pray thee, how He doth not even yet give the whole to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither doth He as yet bestow upon them foreknowledge, so as to learn who is worthy, and who is not so; but He bids them inquire, and await the trial.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then did He Himself abide with a publican?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because he was become worthy by his conversion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And mark, I pray thee, how when He had stripped them of all, He gave them all, by suffering them to abide in the houses of those who became disciples, and to enter therein, having nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thus both themselves were freed from anxiety, and they would convince the others, that for their salvation only are they come; first by bringing in nothing with them, then by requiring no more of them than necessaries, lastly, by not entering all their houses without distinction.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since not by the signs only did He desire them to appear illustrious, but even before the signs, by their own virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For nothing so much characterizes strictness of life,as to be free from superfluities, and so far as may be, from wants.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This even the false apostles knew.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore Paul also said, “That wherein they glory, they may be found even as we.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if when we are in a strange country, and are going unto persons unknown to us, we must seek nothing more than our food for the day, much more when abiding at home.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These things let us not hear only, but also imitate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not of the apostles alone are they said, but also of the saints afterwards.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us therefore become worthy to entertain them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For according to the disposition of the entertainers this peace both comes and flies away again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not only on the courageous speaking of them that teach, but also on the worthiness of them that receive, doth this effect follow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Neither let us account it a small loss, not to enjoy such peace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this peace the prophet also from of old proclaims, saying, “How beautiful are the feet of them that bring good tidings of peace.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then to explain the value thereof he added, “That bring good tidings of good things.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This peace Christ also declared to be great, when He said, “Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And we should do all things, so as to enjoy it, both at home and in church.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in the very church too the presiding minister gives peace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this which we speak of is a type of that.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And you should receive it with all alacrity, in heartbefore the actual communion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if not to impart it after the communionbe disgusting, how much more disgusting to repel from you him that pronounces it!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thee the presbyter sits, for thee the teacher stands, laboring and toiling.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What plea then wilt thou have, for not affording him so much welcome as to listen to Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed the church is the common home of all, and when ye have first occupied it, we enter in, strictly observing the type which they exhibited.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause we also pronounce “peace” in common to all, directly as we enter, according to that law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let no one therefore be careless, no one inattentive,when the priests have entered in and are teaching; for there is really no small punishment appointed for this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, and I for one would rather enter into any of your houses ten thousand times, and find myself baffled, than not be heard when I speak here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This latter is to me harder to bear than the other, by how much this house is of greater dignity; our great possessions being verily laid up here, here all the hopes we have.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what is here, that is not great and awful?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus both this table is far more precious and delightful than the other,and this candlestick than the candlestick there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this they know, as many as have put away diseases by anointing themselves with oilin faith and in due season.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this coffer too is far better and more indispensable than that other chest; for it hath not clothes but alms shut up in it; even though they be few that own them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Here too is a couch better than that other; for the repose of the divine Scriptures is more delightful than any couch.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And had we attained to excellence in respect of concord, then had we no other home beside this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that there is nothing over-burdensome in this saying, the “three thousand,”bear witness, and the “five thousand,”who had but one home, one table, one soul; for “the multitude of them that believed,” we read, “were of one heart and of one soul.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But since we fall far short of their virtue, and dwell scattered in our several homes, let us at least, when we meet here, be earnest in so doing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because though in all other things we be destitute and poor, yet in these we are rich.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore here at least receive us with love when we come in unto you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when I say, “Peace be unto you,”and ye say, “And with thy spirit,” say it not with the voice only, but also with the mind; not in mouth, but in understanding also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if, while here thou sayest, “Peace also to thy spirit,” out of doors thou art mine enemy, spitting at and calumniating me, and secretly aspersing me with innumerable reproaches; what manner of peace is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I indeed, though thou speak evil of me ten thousand times, give thee that peace with a pure heart, with sincerity of purpose, and I can say nothing evil at any time of thee; for I have a father’s bowels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if I rebuke thee at any time, I do it out of concern for thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But as for thee, by thy secret carping at me, and not receiving me in the Lord’s house, I fear lest thou shouldest in return add to my despondency; not for thine insulting me, not for thy casting me out, but for thy rejecting our peace, and drawing down upon thyself that grievous punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though I shake not off the dust, though I turn not away, what is threatened remains unchanged.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I indeed oftentimes pronounce peace to you, and will not cease from continually speaking it; and if, besides your insults, ye receive me not, even then I shake not off the dust; not that I am disobedient to our Lord, but that I vehemently burn for you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And besides, I have suffered nothing at all for you; I have neither come a long journey, nor with that garb and that voluntary poverty am I come (therefore we first blame ourselves), nor without shoes and a second coat; and perhaps this is why ye also fail of your part.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: However, this is not a sufficient plea for you; but while our condemnation is greater, to you it imparts no excuse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then the houses were churches, but now the church is become a house.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then one might say nothing worldly in a house, now one may say nothing spiritual in a church, but even here ye bring in the business from the market place, and while God is discoursing, ye leave off listening in silence to His sayings, and bring in the contrary things, and make discord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And I would it were your own affairs, but now the things which are nothing to you, those ye both speak and hear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this I lament, and will not cease lamenting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I have no power to quit this house, but here we must needs remain until we depart from this present life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Receive us”therefore, as Paul commanded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For his language in that place related not to a meal, but to the temper and mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This we also seek of you, even love, that fervent and genuine affection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if ye endure not even this, at least love yourselves, and lay aside your present remissness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This is sufficient for our consolation, if we see you approving yourselves, and becoming better men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So will I also myself show forth increased love, even “though the more abundantly I love you, the less I be loved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed there are many things to bind us together.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: One table is set before all, one Father begat us, we are all the issue of the same throes, the same drink hath been given to all; or rather not only the same drink, but also to drink out of one cup.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For our Father desiring to lead us to a kindly affection, hath devised this also, that we should drink out of one cup; a thing which belongs to intense love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But “there is no comparison between the apostles and us.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I confess it too, and would never deny it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I say not, to themselves, but not even to their shadows are we comparable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But nevertheless, let your part be done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This will have no tendency to disgrace you but rather to profit you the more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when even to unworthy persons ye show so much love and obedience, then shall ye receive the greater reward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither are they our own words which we speak, since ye have no teacher at all on earth; but what we have received, that we also give, and in giving we seek for nothing else from you, but to be loved only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if we be unworthy even of this, yet by our loving you we shall quickly be worthy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Although we are commanded to love not them only that love us, but even our enemies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who then is so hardhearted, who so savage, that after having received such a law, he should abhor and hate even them that love him, full as he may be of innumerable evils?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: We have partaken of a spiritual table, let us be partakers also of spiritual love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if robbers, on partaking of salt, forget their character; what excuse shall we have, who are continually partaking of the Lord’s body, and do not imitate even their gentleness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet to many, not one table only, but even to be of one city, hath sufficed for friendship; but we, when we have the same city, and the same house, and table, and way, and door, and root, and life, and head, and the same shepherd, and king, and teacher, and judge, and maker, and father, and to whom all things are common; what indulgence can we deserve, if we be divided one from another?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the miracles, perhaps, are what ye seek after, such as they wrought when they entered in; the lepers cleansed, the devils driven out, and the dead raised?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, but this is the great indication of your high birth, and of your love, that ye should believe God without pledges.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And in fact this, and one other thing, were the reasons why God made miracles to cease.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I mean, that if when miracles are not performed, they that plume themselves on other advantages,—for instance, either on the word of wisdom, or on show of piety,—grow vainglorious, are puffed up, are separated one from another; did miracles also take place, how could there but be violent rendings?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that what I say is not mere conjecture, the Corinthians bear witness, who from this cause were divided into many parties.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do not thou therefore seek signs, but the soul’s health.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seek not to see one dead man raised; nay, for thou hast learnt that the whole world is arising.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seek not to see a blind man healed, but behold all now restored unto that better and more profitable sight; and do thou too learn to look chastely, and amend thine eye.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in truth, if we all lived as we ought, workers of miracles would not be admired so much as we by the children of the heathen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as to the signs, they often carry with them either a notion of mere fancy, or another evil suspicion, although ours be not such.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But a pure life cannot admit of any such reproach; yea, all men’s mouths are stopped by the acquisition of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let virtue then be our study: for abundant are her riches, and great the wonder wrought in her.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: She bestows the true freedom, and causes the same to be discerned even in slavery, not releasing from slavery, but while men continue slaves, exhibiting them more honorable than freemen; which is much more than giving them freedom: not making the poor man rich, but while he continues poor, exhibiting him wealthier than the rich.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou wouldest work miracles also, be rid of transgressions, and thou hast quite accomplished it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, for sin is a great demon, beloved; and if thou exterminate this, thou hast wrought a greater thing than they who drive out ten thousand demons.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do thou listen to Paul, how he speaks, and prefers virtue to miracles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But covet earnestly,” saith he, “the best gifts: and yet show I unto you a more excellent way.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when he was to declare this “way,” he spoke not of raising the dead, not of cleansing of lepers, not of any other such thing; but in place of all these he set charity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hearken also unto Christ, saying, “Rejoice not that the demons obey you, but that your names are written in Heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again before this, “Many will say to me in that day, Have we not prophesied in Thy name, and cast out devils, and done many mighty works, and then I will profess unto them, I know you not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when He was about to be crucified, He called His disciples, and said unto them, “By this shall all men know that ye are my disciples,” not “if ye cast out devils,” but “if ye have love one to another.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again, “Hereby shall all men know that Thou hast sent me;” not “if these men raise the dead,” but, “if they be one.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, as to miracles, they oftentimes, while they profited another, have injured him who had the power, by lifting him up to pride and vainglory, or haply in some other way: but in our works there is no place for any such suspicion, but they profit both such as follow them, and many others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These then let us perform with much diligence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if thou change from inhumanity to almsgiving, thou hast stretched forth the hand that was withered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If thou withdraw from theatres and go to the church, thou hast cured the lame foot.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If thou draw back thine eyes from an harlot, and from beauty not thine own, thou hast opened them when they were blind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If instead of satanical songs, thou hast learnt spiritual psalms, being dumb, thou hast spoken.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These are the greatest miracles, these the wonderful signs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If we go on working these signs, we shall both ourselves be a great and admirable sort of persons through these, and shall win over all the wicked unto virtue, and shall enjoy the life to come; unto which may we all attain, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might forever and ever.
